decisions.summary: The shared action system already exists: lib/issueActions.ts (ISSUE_ACTIONS catalog + gating predicates + phase map), useIssueActions.ts (hook computing IssueActionState from the store), and IssueActionMenu.tsx (renders inline/overflow/hybrid). All four named surfaces already render IssueActionMenu — Board (KanbanBoard, mode=hybrid), Cockpit (Stage/IssueStatusBand, mode=hybrid), Pipeline rows (PipelineView, mode=overflow-only), IssueDrawer (DrawerActionBar, mode=hybrid). The work is NOT to build a shared menu from scratch — it is to close the remaining catalog gaps so EVERY action flows through it.

Root cause of the inconsistency: exactly two issue actions bypass the shared catalog. (a) `merge` is not an ISSUE_ACTIONS entry at all — it is the standalone MergeButton component, imported ONLY by drawer/DrawerActionBar.tsx, so an issue is mergeable from the IssueDrawer but NOT from board cards, pipeline rows, or the cockpit. (b) `viewVbrief` (the issue's plan view) is bespoke — a vBRIEF button on KanbanBoard feature cards (opens VBriefDialog) and a separate vBRIEF dig tab in the cockpit, with no presence on pipeline rows or via the shared menu. Every other action the issue enumerates (start, plan, kill=stopAgent, pause, unpause, review=requestReview/restartReview, close=closeOut, sync-main=syncMain, open) is already a first-class catalog action reaching all surfaces.

The existing parity test src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/__tests__/action-parity.test.ts ENSHRINES the gap: it lists `Merge` and `View vBRIEF` in `nonIssueScopedActionLabels` (intentionally outside ISSUE_ACTIONS) and maps `Merge: null` in legacyIssueActionMap. PAN-1610 overturns that classification for the issue-scoped merge and the issue-scoped vBRIEF plan view: both become first-class ISSUE_ACTIONS entries. The parity test must be updated to reflect the new contract (move Merge to legacyIssueActionMap->merge, add viewVbrief) and EXTENDED with a render-level parity assertion proving the same enabled action keys appear on every surface for a ready_to_merge state.

AUTO-MODE DECISION (merge UX): merge is added as an ISSUE_ACTIONS entry with a dialog-host branch that reproduces MergeButton's behavior — a confirm dialog ('Merge <id> to main?'), POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge (endpoint already exists in routes/workspaces.ts:5370, no server work), a dynamic label sourced from reviewStatus.mergeStatus (Merge / Queued / Rebasing / Verifying), and the >2min stuck-merge retry affordance (PAN-490). Gating mirrors MergeButton exactly: enabledWhen = readyForMerge === true && mergeStatus !== 'merged' && canonicalState !== 'verifying_on_main'. Rationale: preserving the existing rich merge feedback while making it a single catalog-driven action is the lowest-risk path to parity without losing UX.

AUTO-MODE DECISION (vBRIEF scope): the catalog gains `viewVbrief` meaning 'open the issue's vBRIEF plan in VBriefDialog', gated enabledWhen = hasPlan. The session-scoped 'View vBRIEF' entry in ZONE_B_SESSION_ACTIONS (a specific agent session's working vBRIEF) is a DIFFERENT concept and stays where it is — it is not an issue-level action. Rationale: the issue asks for parity of issue actions; the issue's plan view is the issue-scoped artifact, the session vBRIEF is agent-session-scoped and correctly out of catalog.

AUTO-MODE DECISION (mode divergence is acceptable, not a gap): hybrid (board/cockpit/drawer) vs overflow-only (pipeline) is intentional layout density — the OVERFLOW menu on every surface exposes the full enabled set, so all valid actions remain reachable everywhere. Parity is defined as 'same set of valid/enabled issue actions reachable', not 'identical button layout'. The deliberate exception is FleetAgentsView (agentScopeOnly) which is the agent-fleet view, NOT one of the four named issue surfaces, and intentionally shows only agent-control actions — left untouched.

AUTO-MODE DECISION (no server changes): the merge endpoint already exists and MergeButton already posts to it. PAN-1610 is a frontend-only refactor confined to src/dashboard/frontend/src. No routes, no contracts, no server services touched.

hazards.summary: Deleting MergeButton.tsx could orphan its 'card' variant which appears unused (only DrawerActionBar imports MergeButton, and only the 'inspector' variant). Before deleting, grep for ALL importers including tests; remove orphaned imports the change creates. Do not delete until DrawerActionBar no longer imports it.
hazards.mitigation: Bead order: refactor DrawerActionBar (B4) BEFORE deleting MergeButton (B6). B6 acceptance criterion includes 'no remaining importers of MergeButton in non-test code'.

hazards.summary: KanbanBoard threads vBRIEF through onViewVBrief callback + setVbriefDialogIssue state on feature cards. Removing the bespoke button must also remove the now-dead callback plumbing AND keep VBriefDialog rendering — but driven by the catalog action's dialog host instead. Risk of leaving a dead VBriefDialog mount or a broken prop chain.
hazards.mitigation: B5 acceptance criteria explicitly require removing the onViewVBrief prop chain and routing vBRIEF viewing through the shared menu's viewVbrief action + dialog host. VBriefDialog stays as the renderer the dialog host opens.

hazards.summary: The stuck-merge retry uses Date.now() against reviewStatus.updatedAt. Any test exercising the >2min stuck threshold MUST use vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.setSystemTime — never real wall-clock (project rule: fake-timers-for-retry-tests).
hazards.mitigation: B2 and B8 acceptance criteria mandate fake timers for the stuck-merge threshold assertion.

hazards.summary: merge confirm + dynamic label means the menu's generic ActionButton is not sufficient — merge needs a dialog-host branch (like open/tell/inspectBead already have) and dynamic view label computation in useIssueActions. Treating merge as a plain safe action with a static label would lose the Queued/Rebasing/Verifying/Retry feedback users rely on.
hazards.mitigation: B2 routes merge through the existing dialog-host branch pattern (activeDialog.key === 'merge') and computes the dynamic label/enabled in useIssueActions from reviewStatus.mergeStatus, mirroring MergeButton.

hazards.summary: Foundation bead B1 changes the IssueActionKey union and ISSUE_ACTIONS shape; every consumer (hook, menu, parity test, surfaces) and ALL downstream beads encode assumptions about the two new keys' names/gating. A wrong key name, gating predicate, or phasePrimary mapping here forces redo of B2-B8.
hazards.mitigation: B1 is requiresInspection:true (deep) with foundationFor listing B2-B8. Inspect that merge/viewVbrief keys, gating predicates, and phase mapping match D4/D5 before downstream work starts.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1610: Consistent issue actions across all surfaces
